quizQuestion: I Love Lucy, the groundbreaking 1951 sitcom starring Lucille Ball, originally aired on which network?
explanation: I Love Lucy premiered on CBS in October 1951 and ran for 6 seasons. Co-created by Lucille Ball and Desi Arnaz, it became one of the most influential sitcoms in TV history and was the first show with a live studio audience recorded on film. Source: Wikipedia/I_Love_Lucy

quizQuestion: Approximately how many Americans watched the I Love Lucy episode 'Lucy Goes to the Hospital' in 1953?
explanation: The 'Lucy Goes to the Hospital' episode drew approximately 44 million viewers when it aired in January 1953 — more people than watched President Eisenhower's inauguration the very next day. It remains one of the most-watched broadcasts in TV history. Source: Wikipedia/I_Love_Lucy

quizQuestion: Who co-created the NBC sitcom Seinfeld alongside Larry David?
explanation: Jerry Seinfeld and Larry David co-created Seinfeld, which ran for 9 seasons on NBC (1989-1998). The show about 'nothing' became one of the most influential sitcoms in TV history, and its 1998 finale was watched by 76.3 million viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Seinfeld

quizQuestion: How many viewers tuned in to watch the Seinfeld series finale in 1998?
explanation: The Seinfeld finale in May 1998 was watched by approximately 76.3 million viewers, making it one of the most-watched TV events in history. Advertisers paid $1 million per 30 seconds of commercial time during the finale. Source: Wikipedia/Seinfeld

quizQuestion: Marta Kauffman co-created Friends with which other television writer?
explanation: Friends was created by David Crane and Marta Kauffman, premiering on NBC in 1994. The show ran for 10 seasons and its 2004 finale drew 52.5 million viewers, making it the most-watched entertainment broadcast of the 2000s decade. Source: Wikipedia/Friends

quizQuestion: How many seasons did Friends run on NBC?
explanation: Friends ran for 10 seasons on NBC from 1994 to 2004. The show about a group of friends living in New York City became one of the most popular sitcoms in television history, with the finale drawing 52.5 million viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Friends

quizQuestion: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air, which launched Will Smith's acting career, originally aired on which network starting in 1990?
explanation: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air premiered on NBC in September 1990 and ran for 6 seasons. Created by Andy and Susan Borowitz, the show launched Will Smith from rapper to international acting superstar. Source: Wikipedia/The_Fresh_Prince_of_Bel-Air

quizQuestion: How many Emmy Awards did Cheers win across its 11-season run on NBC, a record for a comedy series at the time?
explanation: Cheers won 28 Emmy Awards across its 11 seasons on NBC (1982-1993), including Outstanding Comedy Series four times. Created by Glen Charles, Les Charles, and James Burrows, the show's 1993 finale drew 93 million viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Cheers

quizQuestion: Approximately how many viewers watched the Cheers series finale in 1993?
explanation: The Cheers finale in May 1993 attracted approximately 93 million viewers, making it one of the most-watched broadcasts in American TV history. The show ran 11 seasons on NBC and won 28 Emmy Awards. Source: Wikipedia/Cheers

quizQuestion: Approximately how many viewers watched the M*A*S*H series finale in 1983, making it the most-watched broadcast in TV history until 2010?
explanation: The M*A*S*H finale 'Goodbye, Farewell and Amen' in February 1983 drew approximately 125 million viewers — the most-watched broadcast in American TV history until the 2010 Super Bowl. The show ran 11 seasons on CBS. Source: Wikipedia/M*A*S*H_(TV_series)

quizQuestion: Which cartoonist created The Simpsons, the longest-running animated series in American TV history?
explanation: Matt Groening created The Simpsons, which premiered on Fox in December 1989. With 37+ seasons and counting, it is the longest-running American animated series, and Homer's catchphrase 'D'oh!' was added to the Oxford English Dictionary in 2001. Source: Wikipedia/The_Simpsons

quizQuestion: The Simpsons has aired on which network since its 1989 premiere?
explanation: The Simpsons has aired on Fox since December 1989 and with 37+ seasons it is the longest-running American animated series and the longest-running American scripted primetime television series. Source: Wikipedia/The_Simpsons

quizQuestion: Which showrunner created The Sopranos, the HBO drama credited with launching the 'Second Golden Age of Television'?
explanation: David Chase created The Sopranos, which ran on HBO from 1999 to 2007 for 6 seasons. The show won 21 Emmy Awards and is widely credited with ushering in the 'Second Golden Age of Television' with its cinematic storytelling about a New Jersey mob boss. Source: Wikipedia/The_Sopranos

quizQuestion: Which writer created Breaking Bad, which holds a Guinness World Record as the most critically acclaimed TV show ever?
explanation: Vince Gilligan created Breaking Bad, which aired on AMC from 2008 to 2013 for 5 seasons. The show earned 16 Emmy Awards and holds the Guinness World Record for the highest-rated TV series, with a Metacritic score of 99/100. Source: Wikipedia/Breaking_Bad

quizQuestion: Breaking Bad, creator Vince Gilligan's story of a chemistry teacher turned drug manufacturer, aired on which cable network?
explanation: Breaking Bad aired on AMC from 2008 to 2013. AMC, originally a classic movies channel, transformed into a prestige drama powerhouse with Breaking Bad and Mad Men airing in the same era, winning a combined 26+ Emmy Awards. Source: Wikipedia/Breaking_Bad

quizQuestion: Which former Baltimore Sun journalist created The Wire, the HBO drama now widely considered the greatest TV show ever made?
explanation: David Simon, a former Baltimore Sun crime reporter, created The Wire, which aired on HBO from 2002 to 2008 for 5 seasons. Despite low initial ratings, it is now frequently ranked as the greatest TV drama ever made for its unflinching portrayal of Baltimore institutions. Source: Wikipedia/The_Wire

quizQuestion: How many consecutive years did Mad Men win the Outstanding Drama Emmy Award, from 2008 to 2011?
explanation: Mad Men, created by Matthew Weiner, won the Outstanding Drama Series Emmy Award four consecutive years from 2008 to 2011 — an unprecedented achievement at the time. The AMC series ran for 7 seasons from 2007 to 2015. Source: Wikipedia/Mad_Men

quizQuestion: How many Emmy Awards did Game of Thrones win in total, more than any other drama series in history?
explanation: Game of Thrones won 59 Emmy Awards across its 8-season run (2011-2019) on HBO — the most Emmy wins ever for a drama series. Created by David Benioff and D.B. Weiss, the final season averaged 11.99 million viewers per episode. Source: Wikipedia/Game_of_Thrones

quizQuestion: Matt Stone co-created South Park on Comedy Central with which other writer and animator?
explanation: Trey Parker and Matt Stone created South Park, which premiered on Comedy Central in August 1997 and has run for 28+ seasons. The show is famous for producing episodes within approximately one week of their air date, often satirizing current events. Source: Wikipedia/South_Park

quizQuestion: Which voice actor and writer created Family Guy, which famously premiered after Super Bowl XXXIII in 1999?
explanation: Seth MacFarlane created Family Guy, which premiered on Fox in January 1999 after Super Bowl XXXIII. The show was canceled twice before becoming a hit through DVD sales and Adult Swim reruns, eventually running for 24+ seasons. Source: Wikipedia/Family_Guy

quizQuestion: Rick and Morty, created by Justin Roiland and Dan Harmon, originally premiered on which late-night animation block in 2013?
explanation: Rick and Morty premiered on Adult Swim (Cartoon Network's late-night block) in December 2013. The show's cultural impact was demonstrated when McDonald's brought back Szechuan Sauce in 2017 after fans cited a Rick and Morty episode as the reason it should return. Source: Wikipedia/Rick_and_Morty

quizQuestion: Avatar: The Last Airbender, created by DiMartino and Konietzko, originally aired on which children's network starting in 2005?
explanation: Avatar: The Last Airbender aired on Nickelodeon from 2005 to 2008 for 3 seasons. Decades after its original run, the show debuted on Netflix and immediately reached #1, demonstrating its enduring popularity with multiple generations of viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Avatar:_The_Last_Airbender

quizQuestion: Which marine science educator turned animator created SpongeBob SquarePants for Nickelodeon in 1999?
explanation: Stephen Hillenburg, a marine science educator who later studied animation, created SpongeBob SquarePants, which premiered on Nickelodeon in 1999. The franchise has generated over $16 billion in merchandise revenue, making it one of the most profitable animated properties in history. Source: Wikipedia/SpongeBob_SquarePants

quizQuestion: Approximately how many viewers watched the Survivor: The Australian Outback premiere in 2001, a record for the series?
explanation: The Survivor: The Australian Outback premiere drew approximately 45.37 million viewers in January 2001, the highest-rated episode in the show's history. Survivor, created by Charlie Parsons, premiered on CBS in 2000 and has run for 50+ seasons. Source: Wikipedia/Survivor_(American_TV_series)

quizQuestion: How many consecutive seasons was American Idol the #1 show in America, from 2004?
explanation: American Idol was the #1 rated show in America for 7 consecutive seasons starting from 2004. Created by Simon Fuller, the show premiered on Fox in 2002 and later moved to ABC after a hiatus. Source: Wikipedia/American_Idol

quizQuestion: How many consecutive Emmy Awards for Outstanding Host did RuPaul win for hosting RuPaul's Drag Race?
explanation: RuPaul won 8 consecutive Emmy Awards for Outstanding Host for a Reality or Reality-Competition Program for RuPaul's Drag Race, which premiered on Logo TV in 2009 before moving to VH1. The show is produced by World of Wonder. Source: Wikipedia/RuPaul's_Drag_Race

quizQuestion: Stranger Things, created by the Duffer Brothers and set in 1980s Indiana, is an original series exclusive to which streaming platform?
explanation: Stranger Things premiered on Netflix in July 2016, created by Matt and Ross Duffer (the Duffer Brothers). The show's nostalgic 1980s setting and supernatural storylines made it a global hit, and in Season 4 it caused Kate Bush's 1985 song 'Running Up That Hill' to surge back to #1 on music charts worldwide. Source: Wikipedia/Stranger_Things

quizQuestion: Which South Korean filmmaker created Squid Game, Netflix's most-watched series of all time?
explanation: Hwang Dong-hyuk created Squid Game, which premiered on Netflix in September 2021 and became the platform's most-watched series ever. The show won 6 Emmy Awards, including the first Emmy for a non-English-language drama series. Source: Wikipedia/Squid_Game

quizQuestion: In its first week on Netflix in November 2022, approximately how many hours was the Wednesday TV series streamed?
explanation: Wednesday, created by Alfred Gough and Miles Millar and directed by Tim Burton, was streamed for 341 million hours in its first week on Netflix in November 2022 — a record at the time. The show reached #1 in 91 countries. Source: Wikipedia/Wednesday_(TV_series)

quizQuestion: The Mandalorian, created by Jon Favreau and featuring 'Baby Yoda', is a streaming original exclusive to which platform?
explanation: The Mandalorian premiered on Disney+ in November 2019 as a launch title for the new streaming service. Created by Jon Favreau, it uses revolutionary StageCraft LED volume technology and introduced 'Baby Yoda' (Grogu), who became a massive cultural phenomenon. Source: Wikipedia/The_Mandalorian

quizQuestion: Approximately how many viewers watched Tiger King on Netflix within the first 10 days of its release in March 2020?
explanation: Tiger King drew approximately 34.3 million viewers within its first 10 days on Netflix in March 2020, during the early COVID-19 lockdowns. The documentary about exotic animal owner Joe Exotic became a viral cultural phenomenon almost overnight. Source: Wikipedia/Tiger_King

quizQuestion: In what year did Lost premiere on ABC, winning the Emmy for Outstanding Drama in 2005?
explanation: Lost premiered on ABC in September 2004, created by J.J. Abrams, Jeffrey Lieber, and Damon Lindelof. The mystery drama about plane crash survivors on a mysterious island ran for 6 seasons and won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series in 2005. Source: Wikipedia/Lost_(TV_series)

quizQuestion: Which American writer adapted the British version of The Office for NBC, where it ran for 9 seasons beginning in 2005?
explanation: Greg Daniels adapted the British Office (created by Ricky Gervais and Stephen Merchant) for American audiences on NBC, where it ran from 2005 to 2013 for 9 seasons. The American version won the Emmy for Outstanding Comedy Series in 2006. Source: Wikipedia/The_Office_(American_TV_series)

quizQuestion: Greg Daniels co-created Parks and Recreation with which other writer, who later created The Good Place?
explanation: Parks and Recreation was co-created by Greg Daniels and Michael Schur, premiering on NBC in 2009 and running for 7 seasons. Time magazine named it the #1 TV show of 2012. Michael Schur later created Brooklyn Nine-Nine and The Good Place. Source: Wikipedia/Parks_and_Recreation

quizQuestion: Which writer created Arrested Development, the cult-classic comedy that aired on Fox, was canceled, then revived on Netflix?
explanation: Mitchell Hurwitz created Arrested Development, which premiered on Fox in 2003 and ran for 3 seasons before cancellation, then was revived by Netflix for Season 4 (2013) and Season 5 (2018-2019). Despite low ratings during its original run, it won 5 Emmy Awards including Outstanding Comedy Series. Source: Wikipedia/Arrested_Development_(TV_series)
